568	// ============================================================================
569	// DOPPLER DATA DECODING
570	// ============================================================================
571	
572	// Decode 32-bit doppler output into real and imaginary parts
573	// Format: {doppler_q[15:0], doppler_i[15:0]}
574	assign rx_doppler_real = rx_doppler_output[15:0];
575	assign rx_doppler_imag = rx_doppler_output[31:16];
576	assign rx_doppler_data_valid = rx_doppler_valid;
577	
578	// ============================================================================
579	// DC NOTCH FILTER (post-Doppler-FFT, pre-CFAR)
580	// ============================================================================
581	// Zeros out Doppler bins within ±host_dc_notch_width of DC for BOTH
582	// sub-frames in the dual 16-pt FFT architecture.
583	// doppler_bin[4:0] = {sub_frame, bin[3:0]}:
584	//   Sub-frame 0: bins 0-15,  DC = bin 0,  wrap = bin 15
585	//   Sub-frame 1: bins 16-31, DC = bin 16, wrap = bin 31
586	// notch_width=1 → zero bins {0,16}. notch_width=2 → zero bins
587	// {0,1,15,16,17,31}. etc.
588	// When host_dc_notch_width=0: pass-through (no zeroing).
589	
590	wire dc_notch_active;
591	wire [4:0] dop_bin_unsigned = rx_doppler_bin;
592	wire [3:0] bin_within_sf = dop_bin_unsigned[3:0];
593	assign dc_notch_active = (host_dc_notch_width != 3'd0) &&
594	                          (bin_within_sf < {1'b0, host_dc_notch_width} ||
595	                           bin_within_sf > (4'd15 - {1'b0, host_dc_notch_width} + 4'd1));
596	
597	// Notched Doppler data: zero I/Q when in notch zone, pass through otherwise
598	wire [31:0] notched_doppler_data  = dc_notch_active ? 32'd0 : rx_doppler_output;
599	wire        notched_doppler_valid = rx_doppler_valid;
600	wire [4:0]  notched_doppler_bin   = rx_doppler_bin;
601	wire [5:0]  notched_range_bin     = rx_range_bin;
602	
603	// ============================================================================
604	// CFAR DETECTOR (replaces simple threshold detector)
605	// ============================================================================
606	// Cell-Averaging CFAR with CA/GO/SO modes. When cfg_cfar_enable=0,
607	// falls back to simple magnitude threshold (backward-compatible).
608	// See cfar_ca.v for architecture details.
609	
610	wire cfar_detect_flag;
611	wire cfar_detect_valid;
612	wire [5:0]  cfar_detect_range;
613	wire [4:0]  cfar_detect_doppler;
614	wire [16:0] cfar_detect_magnitude;
615	wire [16:0] cfar_detect_threshold;
616	wire [15:0] cfar_detect_count;
617	wire        cfar_busy_w;
618	wire [7:0]  cfar_status_w;
619	
620	cfar_ca cfar_inst (
621	    .clk(clk_100m_buf),
622	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
623	
624	    // Doppler processor outputs (DC-notch filtered)
625	    .doppler_data(notched_doppler_data),
626	    .doppler_valid(notched_doppler_valid),
627	    .doppler_bin_in(notched_doppler_bin),
628	    .range_bin_in(notched_range_bin),
629	    .frame_complete(rx_frame_complete),
630	
631	    // Configuration
632	    .cfg_guard_cells(host_cfar_guard),
633	    .cfg_train_cells(host_cfar_train),
634	    .cfg_alpha(host_cfar_alpha),
635	    .cfg_cfar_mode(host_cfar_mode),
636	    .cfg_cfar_enable(host_cfar_enable),
637	    .cfg_simple_threshold(host_detect_threshold),
638	
639	    // Detection outputs
640	    .detect_flag(cfar_detect_flag),
641	    .detect_valid(cfar_detect_valid),
642	    .detect_range(cfar_detect_range),
643	    .detect_doppler(cfar_detect_doppler),
644	    .detect_magnitude(cfar_detect_magnitude),
645	    .detect_threshold(cfar_detect_threshold),
646	
647	    // Status
648	    .detect_count(cfar_detect_count),
649	    .cfar_busy(cfar_busy_w),
650	    .cfar_status(cfar_status_w)
651	);
652	
653	// Connect CFAR outputs to existing detection signals
654	// (rx_detect_flag/valid are regs — drive them from CFAR combinationally)
655	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
656	    if (!sys_reset_n) begin
657	        rx_detect_flag  <= 1'b0;
658	        rx_detect_valid <= 1'b0;
659	    end else begin
660	        rx_detect_flag  <= cfar_detect_flag;
661	        rx_detect_valid <= cfar_detect_valid;
662	    end
663	end
664	
665	// ============================================================================
666	// BOARD BRING-UP SELF-TEST (opcode 0x30 trigger, 0x31 readback)
667	// ============================================================================
668	// Exercises key subsystems independently on first power-on.
669	// ADC data input is tied to real ADC data.
670	
671	fpga_self_test self_test_inst (
672	    .clk(clk_100m_buf),
673	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
674	    .trigger(host_self_test_trigger),
675	    .busy(self_test_busy),
676	    .result_valid(self_test_result_valid),
677	    .result_flags(self_test_result_flags),
678	    .result_detail(self_test_result_detail),
679	    .adc_data_in(rx_dbg_adc_i),   // Post-DDC I channel (clk_100m, 16-bit signed)
680	    .adc_valid_in(rx_dbg_adc_valid), // DDC output valid (clk_100m)
681	    .capture_active(self_test_capture_active),
682	    .capture_data(self_test_capture_data),
683	    .capture_valid(self_test_capture_valid)
684	);
685	
686	// Latch self-test results when valid (hold until next trigger)
687	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
688	    if (!sys_reset_n) begin
689	        self_test_flags_latched  <= 5'b00000;
690	        self_test_detail_latched <= 8'd0;
691	    end else begin
692	        if (self_test_result_valid) begin
693	            self_test_flags_latched  <= self_test_result_flags;
694	            self_test_detail_latched <= self_test_result_detail;
695	        end
696	    end
697	end
698	
699	// ============================================================================
700	// DATA PACKING FOR USB
701	// ============================================================================
702	
703	// Range profile from matched filter output (wired through radar_receiver_final)
704	assign usb_range_profile = rx_range_profile;
705	assign usb_range_valid = rx_range_valid;
706	
707	assign usb_doppler_real = rx_doppler_real;
708	assign usb_doppler_imag = rx_doppler_imag;
709	assign usb_doppler_valid = rx_doppler_valid;
710	
711	assign usb_detect_flag  = rx_detect_flag;
712	assign usb_detect_valid = rx_detect_valid;
713	
714	// ============================================================================
715	// USB DATA INTERFACE INSTANTIATION (parametric: FT601 or FT2232H)
716	// ============================================================================
717	
718	generate
719	if (USB_MODE == 0) begin : gen_ft601
720	    // ---- FT601 USB 3.0 (32-bit, 200T premium board) ----
721	    usb_data_interface usb_inst (
722	        .clk(clk_100m_buf),
723	        .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
724	        .ft601_reset_n(sys_reset_ft601_n),
725	        
726	        // Radar data inputs
727	        .range_profile(usb_range_profile),
728	        .range_valid(usb_range_valid),
729	        .doppler_real(usb_doppler_real),
730	        .doppler_imag(usb_doppler_imag),
731	        .doppler_valid(usb_doppler_valid),
732	        .cfar_detection(usb_detect_flag),
733	        .cfar_valid(usb_detect_valid),
734	        
735	        // FT601 Interface
736	        .ft601_data(ft601_data),
737	        .ft601_be(ft601_be),
738	        .ft601_txe_n(ft601_txe_n),
739	        .ft601_rxf_n(ft601_rxf_n),
740	        .ft601_txe(ft601_txe),
741	        .ft601_rxf(ft601_rxf),
742	        .ft601_wr_n(ft601_wr_n),
743	        .ft601_rd_n(ft601_rd_n),
744	        .ft601_oe_n(ft601_oe_n),
745	        .ft601_siwu_n(ft601_siwu_n),
746	        .ft601_srb(ft601_srb),
747	        .ft601_swb(ft601_swb),
748	        .ft601_clk_out(ft601_clk_out),
749	        .ft601_clk_in(ft601_clk_buf),
750	        
751	        // Host command outputs
752	        .cmd_data(usb_cmd_data),
753	        .cmd_valid(usb_cmd_valid),
754	        .cmd_opcode(usb_cmd_opcode),
755	        .cmd_addr(usb_cmd_addr),
756	        .cmd_value(usb_cmd_value),
757	
758	        // Stream control
759	        .stream_control(host_stream_control),
760	
761	        // Status readback inputs
762	        .status_request(host_status_request),
763	        .status_cfar_threshold(host_detect_threshold),
764	        .status_stream_ctrl(host_stream_control),
765	        .status_radar_mode(host_radar_mode),
766	        .status_long_chirp(host_long_chirp_cycles),
767	        .status_long_listen(host_long_listen_cycles),
768	        .status_guard(host_guard_cycles),
769	        .status_short_chirp(host_short_chirp_cycles),
770	        .status_short_listen(host_short_listen_cycles),
771	        .status_chirps_per_elev(host_chirps_per_elev),
772	        .status_range_mode(host_range_mode),
773	
774	        // Self-test status readback
775	        .status_self_test_flags(self_test_flags_latched),
776	        .status_self_test_detail(self_test_detail_latched),
777	        .status_self_test_busy(self_test_busy),
778	
779	        // AGC status readback
780	        .status_agc_current_gain(rx_agc_current_gain),
781	        .status_agc_peak_magnitude(rx_agc_peak_magnitude),
782	        .status_agc_saturation_count(rx_agc_saturation_count),
783	        .status_agc_enable(host_agc_enable)
784	    );
785	
786	    // FT2232H ports unused in FT601 mode — tie off
787	    assign ft_rd_n = 1'b1;
788	    assign ft_wr_n = 1'b1;
789	    assign ft_oe_n = 1'b1;
790	    assign ft_siwu = 1'b0;
791	
792	end else begin : gen_ft2232h
793	    // ---- FT2232H USB 2.0 (8-bit, 50T production board) ----
794	    usb_data_interface_ft2232h usb_inst (
795	        .clk(clk_100m_buf),
796	        .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
797	        .ft_reset_n(sys_reset_ft601_n),  // Reuse same synchronized reset
798	        
799	        // Radar data inputs
800	        .range_profile(usb_range_profile),
801	        .range_valid(usb_range_valid),
802	        .doppler_real(usb_doppler_real),
803	        .doppler_imag(usb_doppler_imag),
804	        .doppler_valid(usb_doppler_valid),
805	        .cfar_detection(usb_detect_flag),
806	        .cfar_valid(usb_detect_valid),
807	
808	        // FT2232H Interface
809	        .ft_data(ft_data),
810	        .ft_rxf_n(ft_rxf_n),
811	        .ft_txe_n(ft_txe_n),
812	        .ft_rd_n(ft_rd_n),
813	        .ft_wr_n(ft_wr_n),
814	        .ft_oe_n(ft_oe_n),
815	        .ft_siwu(ft_siwu),
816	        .ft_clk(ft601_clk_buf),   // Reuse BUFG'd USB clock
817	
818	        // Host command outputs
819	        .cmd_data(usb_cmd_data),
820	        .cmd_valid(usb_cmd_valid),
821	        .cmd_opcode(usb_cmd_opcode),
822	        .cmd_addr(usb_cmd_addr),
823	        .cmd_value(usb_cmd_value),
824	
825	        // Stream control
826	        .stream_control(host_stream_control),
827	
828	        // Status readback inputs
829	        .status_request(host_status_request),
830	        .status_cfar_threshold(host_detect_threshold),
831	        .status_stream_ctrl(host_stream_control),
832	        .status_radar_mode(host_radar_mode),
833	        .status_long_chirp(host_long_chirp_cycles),
834	        .status_long_listen(host_long_listen_cycles),
835	        .status_guard(host_guard_cycles),
836	        .status_short_chirp(host_short_chirp_cycles),
837	        .status_short_listen(host_short_listen_cycles),
838	        .status_chirps_per_elev(host_chirps_per_elev),
839	        .status_range_mode(host_range_mode),
840	
841	        // Self-test status readback
842	        .status_self_test_flags(self_test_flags_latched),
843	        .status_self_test_detail(self_test_detail_latched),
844	        .status_self_test_busy(self_test_busy),
845	
846	        // AGC status readback
847	        .status_agc_current_gain(rx_agc_current_gain),
848	        .status_agc_peak_magnitude(rx_agc_peak_magnitude),
849	        .status_agc_saturation_count(rx_agc_saturation_count),
850	        .status_agc_enable(host_agc_enable)
851	    );
852	
853	    // FT601 ports unused in FT2232H mode — tie off
854	    assign ft601_be     = 4'b0000;
855	    assign ft601_txe_n  = 1'b1;
856	    assign ft601_rxf_n  = 1'b1;
857	    assign ft601_wr_n   = 1'b1;
858	    assign ft601_rd_n   = 1'b1;
859	    assign ft601_oe_n   = 1'b1;
860	    assign ft601_siwu_n = 1'b1;
861	    assign ft601_clk_out = 1'b0;
862	end
863	endgenerate
864	
865	// ============================================================================
866	// USB COMMAND CDC: ft601_clk → clk_100m (Gap 4: USB Read Path)
867	// ============================================================================
868	// cmd_valid is a 1-cycle pulse in ft601_clk. Use toggle CDC (same pattern
869	// as chirp_frame_toggle_120m above) to safely transfer it to clk_100m.
870	// cmd_data/opcode/addr/value are held stable after cmd_valid pulses, so
871	// we simply sample them in clk_100m when the CDC'd pulse arrives.
872	
873	// Step 1: Toggle on cmd_valid pulse (ft601_clk domain)
874	reg cmd_valid_toggle_ft601;
875	always @(posedge ft601_clk_buf or negedge sys_reset_ft601_n) begin
876	    if (!sys_reset_ft601_n)
877	        cmd_valid_toggle_ft601 <= 1'b0;
878	    else if (usb_cmd_valid)
879	        cmd_valid_toggle_ft601 <= ~cmd_valid_toggle_ft601;
880	end
881	
882	// Step 2: Synchronize toggle to clk_100m domain (3-stage)
883	wire cmd_valid_toggle_100m;
884	cdc_single_bit #(
885	    .STAGES(3)
886	) cdc_cmd_valid (
887	    .src_clk(ft601_clk_buf),
888	    .dst_clk(clk_100m_buf),
889	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
890	    .src_signal(cmd_valid_toggle_ft601),
891	    .dst_signal(cmd_valid_toggle_100m)
892	);
893	
894	// Step 3: Edge-detect toggle to recover pulse in clk_100m domain
895	reg cmd_valid_toggle_100m_prev;
896	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
897	    if (!sys_reset_n)
898	        cmd_valid_toggle_100m_prev <= 1'b0;
899	    else
900	        cmd_valid_toggle_100m_prev <= cmd_valid_toggle_100m;
901	end
902	wire cmd_valid_100m = cmd_valid_toggle_100m ^ cmd_valid_toggle_100m_prev;
903	
904	// Step 4: Command decode registers in clk_100m domain
905	// Sample cmd_data fields when CDC'd valid pulse arrives. Data is stable
906	// because the read FSM holds cmd_opcode/addr/value until the next command.
907	// NOTE: reg declarations for host_radar_mode, host_trigger_pulse,
908	// host_detect_threshold, host_stream_control are in INTERNAL SIGNALS section
909	// above (before rx_inst) to avoid Icarus Verilog forward-reference errors.
910	
911	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
912	    if (!sys_reset_n) begin
913	        host_radar_mode    <= 2'b01;   // Default: auto-scan
914	        host_trigger_pulse <= 1'b0;
915	        host_detect_threshold <= 16'd10000; // Default threshold
916	        host_stream_control <= 3'b111;    // Default: all streams enabled
917	        host_gain_shift     <= 4'd0;      // Default: pass-through (no gain change)
918	        // Gap 2: chirp timing defaults (match radar_mode_controller parameters)
919	        host_long_chirp_cycles  <= 16'd3000;
920	        host_long_listen_cycles <= 16'd13700;
921	        host_guard_cycles       <= 16'd17540;
922	        host_short_chirp_cycles <= 16'd50;
923	        host_short_listen_cycles <= 16'd17450;
924	        host_chirps_per_elev    <= 6'd32;
925	        host_status_request     <= 1'b0;
926	        chirps_mismatch_error   <= 1'b0;
927	        host_range_mode         <= 2'b00;     // Default: auto
928	        // CFAR defaults (disabled by default — backward-compatible)
929	        host_cfar_guard         <= 4'd2;      // 2 guard cells each side
930	        host_cfar_train         <= 5'd8;      // 8 training cells each side
931	        host_cfar_alpha         <= 8'h30;     // alpha=3.0 (Q4.4)
932	        host_cfar_mode          <= 2'b00;     // CA-CFAR
933	        host_cfar_enable        <= 1'b0;      // Disabled (simple threshold)
934	        // Ground clutter removal defaults (disabled — backward-compatible)
935	        host_mti_enable         <= 1'b0;      // MTI off
936	        host_dc_notch_width     <= 3'd0;      // DC notch off
937	        // AGC defaults (disabled — backward-compatible with manual gain)
938	        host_agc_enable         <= 1'b0;      // AGC off (manual gain)
939	        host_agc_target         <= 8'd200;    // Target peak magnitude
940	        host_agc_attack         <= 4'd1;      // 1-step gain-down on clipping
941	        host_agc_decay          <= 4'd1;      // 1-step gain-up when weak
942	        host_agc_holdoff        <= 4'd4;      // 4 frames before gain-up
943	        // Self-test defaults
944	        host_self_test_trigger  <= 1'b0;      // Self-test idle
945	    end else begin
946	        host_trigger_pulse <= 1'b0;    // Self-clearing pulse
947	        host_status_request <= 1'b0;   // Self-clearing pulse
948	        host_self_test_trigger <= 1'b0;  // Self-clearing pulse
949	        if (cmd_valid_100m) begin
950	            case (usb_cmd_opcode)
951	                8'h01: host_radar_mode     <= usb_cmd_value[1:0];
952	                8'h02: host_trigger_pulse  <= 1'b1;
953	                8'h03: host_detect_threshold <= usb_cmd_value;
954	                8'h04: host_stream_control <= usb_cmd_value[2:0];
955	                // Gap 2: chirp timing configuration
956	                8'h10: host_long_chirp_cycles  <= usb_cmd_value;
957	                8'h11: host_long_listen_cycles <= usb_cmd_value;
958	                8'h12: host_guard_cycles       <= usb_cmd_value;
959	                8'h13: host_short_chirp_cycles <= usb_cmd_value;
960	                8'h14: host_short_listen_cycles <= usb_cmd_value;
961	                8'h15: begin
962	                    // Fix 4: Clamp chirps_per_elev to the fixed Doppler frame size.
963	                    // If host requests a different value, clamp and set error flag.
964	                    if (usb_cmd_value[5:0] > DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS[5:0]) begin
965	                        host_chirps_per_elev  <= DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS[5:0];
966	                        chirps_mismatch_error <= 1'b1;
967	                    end else if (usb_cmd_value[5:0] == 6'd0) begin
968	                        host_chirps_per_elev  <= DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS[5:0];
969	                        chirps_mismatch_error <= 1'b1;
970	                    end else begin
971	                        host_chirps_per_elev  <= usb_cmd_value[5:0];
972	                        // Clear error only if value matches FFT size exactly
973	                        chirps_mismatch_error <= (usb_cmd_value[5:0] != DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS[5:0]);
974	                    end
975	                end
976	                8'h16: host_gain_shift         <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];  // Fix 3: digital gain
977	                8'h20: host_range_mode         <= usb_cmd_value[1:0];  // Fix 7: range mode
978	                // CFAR configuration opcodes
979	                8'h21: host_cfar_guard         <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];
980	                8'h22: host_cfar_train         <= usb_cmd_value[4:0];
981	                8'h23: host_cfar_alpha         <= usb_cmd_value[7:0];
982	                8'h24: host_cfar_mode          <= usb_cmd_value[1:0];
983	                8'h25: host_cfar_enable        <= usb_cmd_value[0];
984	                // Ground clutter removal opcodes
985	                8'h26: host_mti_enable         <= usb_cmd_value[0];
986	                8'h27: host_dc_notch_width     <= usb_cmd_value[2:0];
987	                // AGC configuration opcodes
988	                8'h28: host_agc_enable         <= usb_cmd_value[0];
989	                8'h29: host_agc_target         <= usb_cmd_value[7:0];
990	                8'h2A: host_agc_attack         <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];
991	                8'h2B: host_agc_decay          <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];
992	                8'h2C: host_agc_holdoff        <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];
993	                // Board bring-up self-test opcodes
994	                8'h30: host_self_test_trigger  <= 1'b1;  // Trigger self-test
995	                8'h31: host_status_request     <= 1'b1;  // Self-test readback (status alias)
996	                // 0x31: readback handled via status mechanism (latched results)
997	                8'hFF: host_status_request     <= 1'b1;  // Gap 2: status readback
998	                default: ;
999	            endcase
1000	        end
1001	    end
1002	end
1003	
1004	// ============================================================================
1005	// OUTPUT ASSIGNMENTS
1006	// ============================================================================
1007	
1008	assign current_elevation = tx_current_elevation;
1009	assign current_azimuth = tx_current_azimuth;
1010	assign current_chirp = tx_current_chirp_sync;        // Use CDC-synchronized version
1011	assign new_chirp_frame = tx_new_chirp_frame_sync;    // Use CDC-synchronized version
1012	
1013	assign dbg_doppler_data = rx_doppler_output;
1014	assign dbg_doppler_valid = rx_doppler_valid;
1015	assign dbg_doppler_bin = rx_doppler_bin;
1016	assign dbg_range_bin = rx_range_bin;
1017	
1018	// ============================================================================
1019	// SYSTEM STATUS MONITORING
1020	// ============================================================================
1021	
1022	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
1023	    if (!sys_reset_n) begin
1024	        status_reg <= 4'b0000;
1025	    end else begin
1026	        status_reg[0] <= stm32_mixers_enable_100m;  // Mixers enabled (CDC sync'd)
1027	        status_reg[1] <= ft601_txe_100m;             // USB TX ready (CDC sync'd)
1028	        status_reg[2] <= rx_doppler_valid;          // Data valid
1029	        status_reg[3] <= tx_new_chirp_frame_sync;   // New chirp frame (CDC-sync'd)
1030	    end
1031	end
1032	
1033	assign system_status = status_reg;
1034	
1035	// ============================================================================
1036	// FPGA→STM32 GPIO OUTPUTS (DIG_5, DIG_6, DIG_7)
1037	// ============================================================================
1038	// DIG_5: AGC saturation flag — high when per-frame saturation_count > 0.
1039	//        STM32 reads PD13 to detect clipping and adjust ADAR1000 VGA gain.
1040	// DIG_6: AGC enable flag — mirrors host_agc_enable so STM32 outer-loop AGC
1041	//        tracks the FPGA register as single source of truth.
1042	// DIG_7: Reserved (tied low for future use).
1043	assign gpio_dig5 = (rx_agc_saturation_count != 8'd0);
1044	assign gpio_dig6 = host_agc_enable;
1045	assign gpio_dig7 = 1'b0;
1046	
1047	// ============================================================================
1048	// DEBUG AND VERIFICATION
1049	// ============================================================================
1050	
1051	`ifdef SIMULATION
1052	// Simulation-only debug monitoring
1053	reg [31:0] debug_cycle_counter;
1054	reg [31:0] data_packet_counter;
1055	
1056	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf) begin
1057	    debug_cycle_counter <= debug_cycle_counter + 1;
1058	    
1059	    if (tx_new_chirp_frame_sync) begin
1060	        $display("[TOP] New chirp frame started at cycle %0d", debug_cycle_counter);
1061	    end
1062	    
1063	    if (rx_doppler_valid) begin
1064	        data_packet_counter <= data_packet_counter + 1;
1065	        if (data_packet_counter < 10) begin
1066	            $display("[TOP] Doppler data[%0d]: bin=%0d, range=%0d, I=%0d, Q=%0d",
1067	                     data_packet_counter, rx_doppler_bin, rx_range_bin,
1068	                     rx_doppler_real, rx_doppler_imag);
1069	        end
1070	    end
1071	    
1072	    if (data_packet_counter == 100) begin
1073	        $display("[TOP] First 100 doppler packets processed");
1074	    end
1075	end
1076	`endif
1077	
1078	endmodule
1079

content.content:
1	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
2	
3	module radar_receiver_final (
4	    input wire clk,           // 100MHz    
5		 input wire reset_n,
6	    
7		// ADC Physical Interface (LVDS Inputs)
8	    input wire [7:0] adc_d_p,        // ADC Data P (LVDS)
9	    input wire [7:0] adc_d_n,        // ADC Data N (LVDS)
10	    input wire adc_dco_p,            // Data Clock Output P (400MHz LVDS)
11	    input wire adc_dco_n,            // Data Clock Output N (400MHz LVDS)
12		 output wire adc_pwdn,
13	    
14	    // Chirp counter from transmitter (for matched filter indexing)
15	    input wire [5:0] chirp_counter,
16	    // Frame-start pulse from transmitter (CDC-synchronized, 1 clk_100m cycle)
17	    input wire tx_frame_start,
18	    
19	    output wire [31:0] doppler_output,
20	    output wire doppler_valid,
21	    output wire [4:0] doppler_bin,
22	    output wire [5:0] range_bin,
23	    
24	    // Matched filter range profile output (for USB)
25	    output wire signed [15:0] range_profile_i_out,
26	    output wire signed [15:0] range_profile_q_out,
27	    output wire range_profile_valid_out,
28	    
29	    // Host command inputs (Gap 4: USB Read Path, CDC-synchronized)
30	    // CDC-synchronized in radar_system_top.v before reaching here
31	    input wire [1:0] host_mode,      // Radar mode: 00=STM32, 01=auto-scan, 10=single-chirp
32	    input wire host_trigger,          // Single-chirp trigger pulse (1 clk cycle)
33	
34	    // Gap 2: Host-configurable chirp timing (CDC-synchronized in radar_system_top.v)
35	    input wire [15:0] host_long_chirp_cycles,
36	    input wire [15:0] host_long_listen_cycles,
37	    input wire [15:0] host_guard_cycles,
38	    input wire [15:0] host_short_chirp_cycles,
39	    input wire [15:0] host_short_listen_cycles,
40	    input wire [5:0]  host_chirps_per_elev,
41	
42	    // Digital gain control (Fix 3: between DDC output and matched filter)
43	    // [3]=direction: 0=amplify(left shift), 1=attenuate(right shift)
44	    // [2:0]=shift amount: 0..7 bits. Default 0 = pass-through.
45	    input wire [3:0] host_gain_shift,
46	
47	    // AGC configuration (opcodes 0x28-0x2C, active only when agc_enable=1)
48	    input wire        host_agc_enable,      // 0x28: 0=manual, 1=auto AGC
49	    input wire [7:0]  host_agc_target,      // 0x29: target peak magnitude
50	    input wire [3:0]  host_agc_attack,      // 0x2A: gain-down step on clipping
51	    input wire [3:0]  host_agc_decay,       // 0x2B: gain-up step when weak
52	    input wire [3:0]  host_agc_holdoff,     // 0x2C: frames before gain-up
53	
54	    // STM32 toggle signals for mode 00 (STM32-driven) pass-through.
55	    // These are CDC-synchronized in radar_system_top.v / radar_transmitter.v
56	    // before reaching this module. In mode 00, the RX mode controller uses
57	    // these to synchronize receiver processing with STM32-timed chirps.
58	    input wire stm32_new_chirp_rx,
59	    input wire stm32_new_elevation_rx,
60	    input wire stm32_new_azimuth_rx,
61	
62	    // CFAR integration: expose Doppler frame_complete to top level
63	    output wire doppler_frame_done_out,
64	
65	    // Ground clutter removal controls
66	    input wire        host_mti_enable,       // 1=MTI active, 0=pass-through
67	    input wire [2:0]  host_dc_notch_width,   // DC notch: zero Doppler bins within ±width of DC
68	
69	    // ADC raw data tap (clk_100m domain, post-DDC, for self-test / debug)
70	    output wire [15:0] dbg_adc_i,            // DDC output I (16-bit signed, 100 MHz)
71	    output wire [15:0] dbg_adc_q,            // DDC output Q (16-bit signed, 100 MHz)
72	    output wire        dbg_adc_valid,        // DDC output valid (100 MHz)
73	
74	    // AGC status outputs (for status readback / STM32 outer loop)
75	    output wire [7:0]  agc_saturation_count, // Per-frame clipped sample count
76	    output wire [7:0]  agc_peak_magnitude,   // Per-frame peak (upper 8 bits)
77	    output wire [3:0]  agc_current_gain      // Effective gain_shift encoding
78	);
79	
80	// ========== INTERNAL SIGNALS ==========
81	wire use_long_chirp;
82	// NOTE: chirp_counter is now an input port (was undriven internal wire — bug NEW-1)
83	wire chirp_start;
84	wire azimuth_change;
85	wire elevation_change;
86	
87	// Mode controller outputs → matched_filter_multi_segment
88	wire mc_new_chirp;
89	wire mc_new_elevation;
90	wire mc_new_azimuth;
91	
92	wire [1:0] segment_request;
93	wire mem_request;
94	wire [15:0] ref_i, ref_q;
95	wire mem_ready;
96	
97	wire [15:0] adc_i_scaled, adc_q_scaled;
98	wire adc_valid_sync;
99	
100	// Gain-controlled signals (between DDC output and matched filter)
101	wire signed [15:0] gc_i, gc_q;
102	wire gc_valid;
103	wire [7:0] gc_saturation_count;  // Diagnostic: per-frame clipped sample counter
104	wire [7:0] gc_peak_magnitude;    // Diagnostic: per-frame peak magnitude
105	wire [3:0] gc_current_gain;      // Diagnostic: effective gain_shift
106	
107	// Reference signals for the processing chain
108	wire [15:0] long_chirp_real, long_chirp_imag;
109	wire [15:0] short_chirp_real, short_chirp_imag;
110	
111	// ========== DOPPLER PROCESSING SIGNALS ==========
112	wire [31:0] range_data_32bit;
113	wire range_data_valid;
114	wire new_chirp_frame;
115	
116	// Doppler processor outputs
117	wire [31:0] doppler_spectrum;
118	wire doppler_spectrum_valid;
119	wire [4:0] doppler_bin_out;
120	wire doppler_processing;
121	wire doppler_frame_done;
122	assign doppler_frame_done_out = doppler_frame_done;
123	
124	// ========== RANGE BIN DECIMATOR SIGNALS ==========
125	wire signed [15:0] decimated_range_i;
126	wire signed [15:0] decimated_range_q;
127	wire decimated_range_valid;
128	wire [5:0] decimated_range_bin;
129	
130	// ========== MTI CANCELLER SIGNALS ==========
131	wire signed [15:0] mti_range_i;
132	wire signed [15:0] mti_range_q;
133	wire mti_range_valid;
134	wire [5:0] mti_range_bin;
135	wire mti_first_chirp;
136	
137	// ========== RADAR MODE CONTROLLER SIGNALS ==========
138	wire rmc_scanning;
139	wire rmc_scan_complete;
140	wire [5:0] rmc_chirp_count;
141	wire [5:0] rmc_elevation_count;
142	wire [5:0] rmc_azimuth_count;
143	
144	// ========== MODULE INSTANTIATIONS ==========
145	
146	// 0. Radar Mode Controller — drives chirp/elevation/azimuth timing signals
147	//    Default mode: auto-scan (2'b01). Change to 2'b00 for STM32 pass-through.
148	radar_mode_controller rmc (
149	    .clk(clk),
150	    .reset_n(reset_n),
151	    .mode(host_mode),                     // Controlled by host via USB (default: 2'b01 auto-scan)
152	    .stm32_new_chirp(stm32_new_chirp_rx),
153	    .stm32_new_elevation(stm32_new_elevation_rx),
154	    .stm32_new_azimuth(stm32_new_azimuth_rx),
155	    .trigger(host_trigger),           // Single-chirp trigger from host via USB
156	    // Gap 2: Runtime-configurable timing from host USB commands
157	    .cfg_long_chirp_cycles(host_long_chirp_cycles),
158	    .cfg_long_listen_cycles(host_long_listen_cycles),
159	    .cfg_guard_cycles(host_guard_cycles),
160	    .cfg_short_chirp_cycles(host_short_chirp_cycles),
161	    .cfg_short_listen_cycles(host_short_listen_cycles),
162	    .cfg_chirps_per_elev(host_chirps_per_elev),
163	    .use_long_chirp(use_long_chirp),
164	    .mc_new_chirp(mc_new_chirp),
165	    .mc_new_elevation(mc_new_elevation),
166	    .mc_new_azimuth(mc_new_azimuth),
167	    .chirp_count(rmc_chirp_count),
168	    .elevation_count(rmc_elevation_count),
169	    .azimuth_count(rmc_azimuth_count),
170	    .scanning(rmc_scanning),
171	    .scan_complete(rmc_scan_complete)
172	);
173	wire clk_400m;
174	
175	// NOTE: lvds_to_cmos_400m removed — ad9484_interface_400m now provides
176	// the buffered 400MHz DCO clock via adc_dco_bufg, avoiding duplicate
177	// IBUFDS instantiations on the same LVDS clock pair.
178	
179	// 1. ADC + CDC + Digital Gain
180	
181	// CMOS Output Interface (400MHz Domain)
182	wire [7:0] adc_data_cmos;  // 8-bit ADC data (CMOS, from ad9484_interface_400m)
183	wire adc_valid;            // Data valid signal
184	
185	// ADC power-down control (directly tie low = ADC always on)
186	assign adc_pwdn = 1'b0;
187	
188	ad9484_interface_400m adc (
189		.adc_d_p(adc_d_p),
190		.adc_d_n(adc_d_n),
191		.adc_dco_p(adc_dco_p),
192		.adc_dco_n(adc_dco_n),
193		.sys_clk(clk),
194		.reset_n(reset_n),
195		.adc_data_400m(adc_data_cmos),
196		.adc_data_valid_400m(adc_valid),
197		.adc_dco_bufg(clk_400m)
198	);
199	
200	// NOTE: The cdc_adc_to_processing instance that was here used src_clk=dst_clk=clk_400m
201	// (same clock domain — no crossing). Gray-code CDC on same-clock with fast-changing
202	// ADC data corrupts samples because Gray coding only guarantees safe transfer of
203	// values that change by 1 LSB at a time. The real 400MHz→100MHz CDC crossing is
204	// handled inside ddc_400m_enhanced via CIC decimation + CDC_FIR instances.
205	// Removed: cdc_adc_to_processing instance. ADC data now goes directly to DDC.
206	
207	// 2. DDC Input Interface
208	wire signed [17:0] ddc_out_i;
209	wire signed [17:0] ddc_out_q;
210	
211	wire ddc_valid_i;
212	wire ddc_valid_q;
213	
214	ddc_400m_enhanced ddc(
215	    .clk_400m(clk_400m),           // 400MHz clock from ADC DCO
216	    .clk_100m(clk),           // 100MHz system clock //used by the 2 FIR
217	    .reset_n(reset_n),
218	    .adc_data(adc_data_cmos),     // ADC data at 400MHz (direct from ADC interface)
219	    .adc_data_valid_i(adc_valid),     // Valid at 400MHz
220	    .adc_data_valid_q(adc_valid),     // Valid at 400MHz
221	    .baseband_i(ddc_out_i), // I output at 100MHz
222	    .baseband_q(ddc_out_q), // Q output at 100MHz  
223	    .baseband_valid_i(ddc_valid_i),     // Valid at 100MHz
224		 .baseband_valid_q(ddc_valid_q),
225	 	 .mixers_enable(1'b1)
226	);
227	
228	ddc_input_interface ddc_if (
229	    .clk(clk),
230	    .reset_n(reset_n),
231	    .ddc_i(ddc_out_i),
232	    .ddc_q(ddc_out_q),
233	    .valid_i(ddc_valid_i),
234	    .valid_q(ddc_valid_q),
235	    .adc_i(adc_i_scaled),
236	    .adc_q(adc_q_scaled),
237	    .adc_valid(adc_valid_sync),
238	    .data_sync_error()
239	);
240	
241	// 2b. Digital Gain Control with AGC
242	// Host-configurable power-of-2 shift between DDC output and matched filter.
243	// Default gain_shift=0, agc_enable=0 → pass-through (no behavioral change).
244	// When agc_enable=1: auto-adjusts gain per frame based on peak/saturation.
245	rx_gain_control gain_ctrl (
246	    .clk(clk),
247	    .reset_n(reset_n),
248	    .data_i_in(adc_i_scaled),
249	    .data_q_in(adc_q_scaled),
250	    .valid_in(adc_valid_sync),
251	    .gain_shift(host_gain_shift),
252	    // AGC configuration
253	    .agc_enable(host_agc_enable),
254	    .agc_target(host_agc_target),
255	    .agc_attack(host_agc_attack),
256	    .agc_decay(host_agc_decay),
257	    .agc_holdoff(host_agc_holdoff),
258	    // Frame boundary from Doppler processor
259	    .frame_boundary(doppler_frame_done),
260	    // Outputs
261	    .data_i_out(gc_i),
262	    .data_q_out(gc_q),
263	    .valid_out(gc_valid),
264	    .saturation_count(gc_saturation_count),
265	    .peak_magnitude(gc_peak_magnitude),
266	    .current_gain(gc_current_gain)
267	);
268	
269	// 3. Dual Chirp Memory Loader
270	wire [9:0] sample_addr_from_chain;
271	
272	chirp_memory_loader_param chirp_mem (
273	    .clk(clk),
274	    .reset_n(reset_n),
275	    .segment_select(segment_request),
276	    .mem_request(mem_request),
277	    .use_long_chirp(use_long_chirp),
278		 .sample_addr(sample_addr_from_chain),
279	    .ref_i(ref_i),
280	    .ref_q(ref_q),
281	    .mem_ready(mem_ready)
282	);
283	
284	// Sample address generator
285	reg [9:0] sample_addr_reg;
286	always @(posedge clk or negedge reset_n) begin
287	    if (!reset_n) begin
288	        sample_addr_reg <= 0;
289	    end else if (mem_request) begin
290	        sample_addr_reg <= sample_addr_reg + 1;
291	        if (sample_addr_reg == 1023) sample_addr_reg <= 0;
292	    end
293	end
294	// sample_addr_wire removed — was unused implicit wire (synthesis warning)
295	
296	// 4. CRITICAL: Reference Chirp Latency Buffer
297	// This aligns reference data with FFT output (2159 cycle delay)
298	wire [15:0] delayed_ref_i, delayed_ref_q;
299	wire mem_ready_delayed;
300	
301	latency_buffer #(
302	    .DATA_WIDTH(32),  // 16-bit I + 16-bit Q
303		.LATENCY(3187)
304	) ref_latency_buffer (
305	    .clk(clk),
306	    .reset_n(reset_n),
307	    .data_in({ref_i, ref_q}),
308	    .valid_in(mem_request),
309	    .data_out({delayed_ref_i, delayed_ref_q}),
310	    .valid_out(mem_ready_delayed)
311	);
312	
313	// Assign delayed reference signals
314	assign long_chirp_real = delayed_ref_i;
315	assign long_chirp_imag = delayed_ref_q;
316	assign short_chirp_real = delayed_ref_i;
317	assign short_chirp_imag = delayed_ref_q;
318	
319	// 5. Dual Chirp Matched Filter
320	
321	wire signed [15:0] range_profile_i;
322	wire signed [15:0] range_profile_q;
323	wire range_valid;
324	
325	// Expose matched filter output to top level for USB range profile
326	assign range_profile_i_out = range_profile_i;
327	assign range_profile_q_out = range_profile_q;
328	assign range_profile_valid_out = range_valid;
329	
330	matched_filter_multi_segment mf_dual (
331	    .clk(clk),
332	    .reset_n(reset_n),
333	    .ddc_i({{2{gc_i[15]}}, gc_i}),
334	    .ddc_q({{2{gc_q[15]}}, gc_q}),
335	    .ddc_valid(gc_valid),
336	    .use_long_chirp(use_long_chirp),
337	    .chirp_counter(chirp_counter),
338	    .mc_new_chirp(mc_new_chirp),
339	    .mc_new_elevation(mc_new_elevation),
340	    .mc_new_azimuth(mc_new_azimuth),
341		 .long_chirp_real(delayed_ref_i),      // From latency buffer
342	    .long_chirp_imag(delayed_ref_q),
343	    .short_chirp_real(delayed_ref_i),     // Same for short chirp
344	    .short_chirp_imag(delayed_ref_q),
345	    .segment_request(segment_request),
346	    .mem_request(mem_request),
347		 .sample_addr_out(sample_addr_from_chain),
348	    .mem_ready(mem_ready),
349	    .pc_i_w(range_profile_i),
350	    .pc_q_w(range_profile_q),
351	    .pc_valid_w(range_valid)
352	);
353	
354	// ========== CRITICAL: RANGE BIN DECIMATOR ==========
355	// Convert 1024 range bins to 64 bins for Doppler
356	range_bin_decimator #(
357	    .INPUT_BINS(1024),
358	    .OUTPUT_BINS(64),
359	    .DECIMATION_FACTOR(16)
360	) range_decim (
361	    .clk(clk),
362	    .reset_n(reset_n),
363	    .range_i_in(range_profile_i),
364	    .range_q_in(range_profile_q),
365	    .range_valid_in(range_valid),
366	    .range_i_out(decimated_range_i),
367	    .range_q_out(decimated_range_q),
368	    .range_valid_out(decimated_range_valid),
369	    .range_bin_index(decimated_range_bin),
370	    .decimation_mode(2'b01),           // Peak detection mode
371	    .start_bin(10'd0),
372	    .watchdog_timeout()                // Diagnostic — unconnected (monitored via ILA if needed)
373	);
374	
375	// ========== MTI CANCELLER (Ground Clutter Removal) ==========
376	// 2-pulse canceller: subtracts previous chirp from current chirp.
377	// H(z) = 1 - z^{-1} → null at DC Doppler, removes stationary clutter.
378	// When host_mti_enable=0: transparent pass-through.
379	mti_canceller #(
380	    .NUM_RANGE_BINS(64),
381	    .DATA_WIDTH(16)
382	) mti_inst (
383	    .clk(clk),
384	    .reset_n(reset_n),
385	    .range_i_in(decimated_range_i),
386	    .range_q_in(decimated_range_q),
387	    .range_valid_in(decimated_range_valid),
388	    .range_bin_in(decimated_range_bin),
389	    .range_i_out(mti_range_i),
390	    .range_q_out(mti_range_q),
391	    .range_valid_out(mti_range_valid),
392	    .range_bin_out(mti_range_bin),
393	    .mti_enable(host_mti_enable),
394	    .mti_first_chirp(mti_first_chirp)
395	);
396	
397	// ========== FRAME SYNC FROM TRANSMITTER ==========
398	// [FPGA-001 FIXED] Use the authoritative new_chirp_frame signal from the
399	// transmitter (via plfm_chirp_controller_enhanced), CDC-synchronized to
400	// clk_100m in radar_system_top.  Previous code tried to derive frame
401	// boundaries from chirp_counter == 0, but that counter comes from the
402	// transmitter path (plfm_chirp_controller_enhanced) which does NOT wrap
403	// at chirps_per_elev — it overflows to N and only wraps at 6-bit rollover
404	// (64).  This caused frame pulses at half the expected rate for N=32.
405	reg tx_frame_start_prev;
406	reg new_frame_pulse;
407	
408	always @(posedge clk or negedge reset_n) begin
409	    if (!reset_n) begin
410	        tx_frame_start_prev <= 1'b0;
411	        new_frame_pulse <= 1'b0;
412	    end else begin
413	        new_frame_pulse <= 1'b0;
414	        
415	        // Edge detect: tx_frame_start is a toggle-CDC derived pulse that
416	        // may be 1 clock wide.  Capture rising edge for clean 1-cycle pulse.
417	        if (tx_frame_start && !tx_frame_start_prev) begin
418	            new_frame_pulse <= 1'b1;
419	        end
420	        
421	        tx_frame_start_prev <= tx_frame_start;
422	    end
423	end
424	
425	assign new_chirp_frame = new_frame_pulse;
426	
427	// ========== DATA PACKING FOR DOPPLER ==========
428	// Use MTI-filtered data (or pass-through if MTI disabled)
429	assign range_data_32bit = {mti_range_q, mti_range_i};
430	assign range_data_valid = mti_range_valid;
431	
432	// ========== DOPPLER PROCESSOR ==========
433	doppler_processor_optimized #(
434	    .DOPPLER_FFT_SIZE(16),
435	    .RANGE_BINS(64),
436	    .CHIRPS_PER_FRAME(32),
437	    .CHIRPS_PER_SUBFRAME(16)
438	) doppler_proc (
439	    .clk(clk),
440	    .reset_n(reset_n),
441	    .range_data(range_data_32bit),
442	    .data_valid(range_data_valid),
443	    .new_chirp_frame(new_chirp_frame),
444	    
445	    // Outputs
446	    .doppler_output(doppler_output),
447	    .doppler_valid(doppler_valid),
448	    .doppler_bin(doppler_bin),
449	    .range_bin(range_bin),
450	    
451	    // Status
452	    .processing_active(doppler_processing),
453	    .frame_complete(doppler_frame_done),
454	    .status()
455	);
456	
457	// ========== OUTPUT CONNECTIONS ==========
458	// doppler_output, doppler_valid, doppler_bin, range_bin are directly
459	// connected to doppler_proc ports above
460	
461	// ========== STATUS ==========
462	
463	// ========== DEBUG AND VERIFICATION ==========
464	reg [31:0] frame_counter;
465	reg [5:0] chirps_in_current_frame;
466	
467	always @(posedge clk or negedge reset_n) begin
468	    if (!reset_n) begin
469	        frame_counter <= 0;
470	        chirps_in_current_frame <= 0;
471	    end else begin
472	        // Count chirps in current frame
473	        if (range_data_valid && decimated_range_bin == 0) begin
474	            // First range bin of a chirp
475	            chirps_in_current_frame <= chirps_in_current_frame + 1;
476	        end
477	        
478	        // Detect frame completion
479	        if (new_chirp_frame) begin
480	            frame_counter <= frame_counter + 1;
481	            `ifdef SIMULATION
482	            $display("[TOP] Frame %0d started. Previous frame had %0d chirps", 
483	                     frame_counter, chirps_in_current_frame);
484	            `endif
485	            chirps_in_current_frame <= 0;
486	        end
487	    end
488	end
489	
490	
491	// ========== ADC DEBUG TAP (for self-test / bring-up) ==========
492	assign dbg_adc_i     = adc_i_scaled;
493	assign dbg_adc_q     = adc_q_scaled;
494	assign dbg_adc_valid = adc_valid_sync;
495	
496	// ========== AGC STATUS OUTPUTS ==========
497	assign agc_saturation_count = gc_saturation_count;
498	assign agc_peak_magnitude   = gc_peak_magnitude;
499	assign agc_current_gain     = gc_current_gain;
500	
501	endmodule
502

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	AERIS-10 Radar Protocol Layer
4	===============================
5	Pure-logic module for USB packet parsing and command building.
6	No GUI dependencies — safe to import from tests and headless scripts.
7	
8	USB Interface: FT2232H USB 2.0 (8-bit, 50T production board) via pyftdi
9	               FT601 USB 3.0 (32-bit, 200T premium board) via ftd3xx
10	
11	USB Packet Protocol (11-byte):
12	  TX (FPGA→Host):
13	    Data packet:  [0xAA] [range_q 2B] [range_i 2B] [dop_re 2B] [dop_im 2B] [det 1B] [0x55]
14	    Status packet: [0xBB] [status 6x32b] [0x55]
15	  RX (Host→FPGA):
16	    Command: 4 bytes received sequentially {opcode, addr, value_hi, value_lo}
17	"""
18	
19	import struct
20	import time
21	import threading
22	import queue
23	import logging
24	import contextlib
25	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
26	from typing import Any, ClassVar
27	from enum import IntEnum
28	
29	
30	import numpy as np
31	
32	log = logging.getLogger("radar_protocol")
33	
34	# ============================================================================
35	# Constants matching usb_data_interface.v
36	# ============================================================================
37	
38	HEADER_BYTE = 0xAA
39	FOOTER_BYTE = 0x55
40	STATUS_HEADER_BYTE = 0xBB
41	
42	# Packet sizes
43	DATA_PACKET_SIZE = 11               # 1 + 4 + 2 + 2 + 1 + 1
44	STATUS_PACKET_SIZE = 26              # 1 + 24 + 1
45	
46	NUM_RANGE_BINS = 64
47	NUM_DOPPLER_BINS = 32
48	NUM_CELLS = NUM_RANGE_BINS * NUM_DOPPLER_BINS  # 2048
49	
50	WATERFALL_DEPTH = 64
51	
52	
53	class Opcode(IntEnum):
54	    """Host register opcodes — must match radar_system_top.v case(usb_cmd_opcode).
55	
56	    FPGA truth table (from radar_system_top.v lines 902-944):
57	        0x01  host_radar_mode        0x14  host_short_listen_cycles
58	        0x02  host_trigger_pulse     0x15  host_chirps_per_elev
59	        0x03  host_detect_threshold  0x16  host_gain_shift
60	        0x04  host_stream_control    0x20  host_range_mode
61	        0x10  host_long_chirp_cycles 0x21-0x27  CFAR / MTI / DC-notch
62	        0x11  host_long_listen_cycles 0x28-0x2C  AGC control
63	        0x12  host_guard_cycles      0x30  host_self_test_trigger
64	        0x13  host_short_chirp_cycles 0x31/0xFF  host_status_request
65	    """
66	    # --- Basic control (0x01-0x04) ---
67	    RADAR_MODE          = 0x01  # 2-bit mode select
68	    TRIGGER_PULSE       = 0x02  # self-clearing one-shot trigger
69	    DETECT_THRESHOLD    = 0x03  # 16-bit detection threshold value
70	    STREAM_CONTROL      = 0x04  # 3-bit stream enable mask
71	
72	    # --- Digital gain (0x16) ---
73	    GAIN_SHIFT          = 0x16  # 4-bit digital gain shift
74	
75	    # --- Chirp timing (0x10-0x15) ---
76	    LONG_CHIRP          = 0x10
77	    LONG_LISTEN         = 0x11
78	    GUARD               = 0x12
79	    SHORT_CHIRP         = 0x13
80	    SHORT_LISTEN        = 0x14
81	    CHIRPS_PER_ELEV     = 0x15
82	
83	    # --- Signal processing (0x20-0x27) ---
84	    RANGE_MODE          = 0x20
85	    CFAR_GUARD          = 0x21
86	    CFAR_TRAIN          = 0x22
87	    CFAR_ALPHA          = 0x23
88	    CFAR_MODE           = 0x24
89	    CFAR_ENABLE         = 0x25
90	    MTI_ENABLE          = 0x26
91	    DC_NOTCH_WIDTH      = 0x27
92	
93	    # --- AGC (0x28-0x2C) ---
94	    AGC_ENABLE          = 0x28
95	    AGC_TARGET          = 0x29
96	    AGC_ATTACK          = 0x2A
97	    AGC_DECAY           = 0x2B
98	    AGC_HOLDOFF         = 0x2C
99	
100	    # --- Board self-test / status (0x30-0x31, 0xFF) ---
101	    SELF_TEST_TRIGGER   = 0x30
102	    SELF_TEST_STATUS    = 0x31
103	    STATUS_REQUEST      = 0xFF
104	
105	
106	# ============================================================================
107	# Data Structures
108	# ============================================================================
109	
110	@dataclass
111	class RadarFrame:
112	    """One complete radar frame (64 range x 32 Doppler)."""
113	    timestamp: float = 0.0
114	    range_doppler_i: np.ndarray = field(
115	        default_factory=lambda: np.zeros((NUM_RANGE_BINS, NUM_DOPPLER_BINS), dtype=np.int16))
116	    range_doppler_q: np.ndarray = field(
117	        default_factory=lambda: np.zeros((NUM_RANGE_BINS, NUM_DOPPLER_BINS), dtype=np.int16))
118	    magnitude: np.ndarray = field(
119	        default_factory=lambda: np.zeros((NUM_RANGE_BINS, NUM_DOPPLER_BINS), dtype=np.float64))
120	    detections: np.ndarray = field(
121	        default_factory=lambda: np.zeros((NUM_RANGE_BINS, NUM_DOPPLER_BINS), dtype=np.uint8))
122	    range_profile: np.ndarray = field(
123	        default_factory=lambda: np.zeros(NUM_RANGE_BINS, dtype=np.float64))
124	    detection_count: int = 0
125	    frame_number: int = 0
126	
127	
128	@dataclass
129	class StatusResponse:
130	    """Parsed status response from FPGA (6-word / 26-byte packet)."""
131	    radar_mode: int = 0
132	    stream_ctrl: int = 0
133	    cfar_threshold: int = 0
134	    long_chirp: int = 0
135	    long_listen: int = 0
136	    guard: int = 0
137	    short_chirp: int = 0
138	    short_listen: int = 0
139	    chirps_per_elev: int = 0
140	    range_mode: int = 0
141	    # Self-test results (word 5, added in Build 26)
142	    self_test_flags: int = 0     # 5-bit result flags [4:0]
143	    self_test_detail: int = 0    # 8-bit detail code [7:0]
144	    self_test_busy: int = 0      # 1-bit busy flag
145	    # AGC metrics (word 4, added for hybrid AGC)
146	    agc_current_gain: int = 0    # 4-bit current gain encoding [3:0]
147	    agc_peak_magnitude: int = 0  # 8-bit peak magnitude [7:0]
148	    agc_saturation_count: int = 0  # 8-bit saturation count [7:0]
149	    agc_enable: int = 0          # 1-bit AGC enable readback
150	
151	
152	# ============================================================================
153	# Protocol: Packet Parsing & Building
154	# ============================================================================
155	
156	def _to_signed16(val: int) -> int:
157	    """Convert unsigned 16-bit integer to signed (two's complement)."""
158	    val = val & 0xFFFF
159	    return val - 0x10000 if val >= 0x8000 else val
160	
161	
162	class RadarProtocol:
163	    """
164	    Parse FPGA→Host packets and build Host→FPGA command words.
165	    Matches usb_data_interface.v packet format exactly.
166	    """
167	
168	    @staticmethod
169	    def build_command(opcode: int, value: int, addr: int = 0) -> bytes:
170	        """
171	        Build a 32-bit command word: {opcode[31:24], addr[23:16], value[15:0]}.
172	        Returns 4 bytes, big-endian (MSB first).
173	        """
174	        word = ((opcode & 0xFF) << 24) | ((addr & 0xFF) << 16) | (value & 0xFFFF)
175	        return struct.pack(">I", word)
176	
177	    @staticmethod
178	    def parse_data_packet(raw: bytes) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
179	        """
180	        Parse an 11-byte data packet from the FT2232H byte stream.
181	        Returns dict with keys: 'range_i', 'range_q', 'doppler_i', 'doppler_q',
182	        'detection', or None if invalid.
183	
184	        Packet format (11 bytes):
185	          Byte 0:    0xAA (header)
186	          Bytes 1-2: range_q[15:0] MSB first
187	          Bytes 3-4: range_i[15:0] MSB first
188	          Bytes 5-6: doppler_real[15:0] MSB first
189	          Bytes 7-8: doppler_imag[15:0] MSB first
190	          Byte 9:    {7'b0, cfar_detection}
191	          Byte 10:   0x55 (footer)
192	        """
193	        if len(raw) < DATA_PACKET_SIZE:
194	            return None
195	        if raw[0] != HEADER_BYTE:
196	            return None
197	        if raw[10] != FOOTER_BYTE:
198	            return None
199	
200	        range_q = _to_signed16(struct.unpack_from(">H", raw, 1)[0])
201	        range_i = _to_signed16(struct.unpack_from(">H", raw, 3)[0])
202	        doppler_i = _to_signed16(struct.unpack_from(">H", raw, 5)[0])
203	        doppler_q = _to_signed16(struct.unpack_from(">H", raw, 7)[0])
204	        det_byte = raw[9]
205	        detection = det_byte & 0x01
206	        frame_start = (det_byte >> 7) & 0x01
207	
208	        return {
209	            "range_i": range_i,
210	            "range_q": range_q,
211	            "doppler_i": doppler_i,
212	            "doppler_q": doppler_q,
213	            "detection": detection,
214	            "frame_start": frame_start,
215	        }
216	
217	    @staticmethod
218	    def parse_status_packet(raw: bytes) -> StatusResponse | None:
219	        """
220	        Parse a status response packet.
221	        Format: [0xBB] [6x4B status words] [0x55] = 1 + 24 + 1 = 26 bytes
222	        """
223	        if len(raw) < 26:
224	            return None
225	        if raw[0] != STATUS_HEADER_BYTE:
226	            return None
227	
228	        words = []
229	        for i in range(6):
230	            w = struct.unpack_from(">I", raw, 1 + i * 4)[0]
231	            words.append(w)
232	
233	        if raw[25] != FOOTER_BYTE:
234	            return None
235	
236	        sr = StatusResponse()
237	        # Word 0: {0xFF[31:24], mode[23:22], stream[21:19], 3'b000[18:16], threshold[15:0]}
238	        sr.cfar_threshold = words[0] & 0xFFFF
239	        sr.stream_ctrl = (words[0] >> 19) & 0x07
240	        sr.radar_mode = (words[0] >> 22) & 0x03
241	        # Word 1: {long_chirp[31:16], long_listen[15:0]}
242	        sr.long_listen = words[1] & 0xFFFF
243	        sr.long_chirp = (words[1] >> 16) & 0xFFFF
244	        # Word 2: {guard[31:16], short_chirp[15:0]}
245	        sr.short_chirp = words[2] & 0xFFFF
246	        sr.guard = (words[2] >> 16) & 0xFFFF
247	        # Word 3: {short_listen[31:16], 10'd0, chirps_per_elev[5:0]}
248	        sr.chirps_per_elev = words[3] & 0x3F
249	        sr.short_listen = (words[3] >> 16) & 0xFFFF
250	        # Word 4: {agc_current_gain[31:28], agc_peak_magnitude[27:20],
251	        #          agc_saturation_count[19:12], agc_enable[11], 9'd0, range_mode[1:0]}
252	        sr.range_mode = words[4] & 0x03
253	        sr.agc_enable = (words[4] >> 11) & 0x01
254	        sr.agc_saturation_count = (words[4] >> 12) & 0xFF
255	        sr.agc_peak_magnitude = (words[4] >> 20) & 0xFF
256	        sr.agc_current_gain = (words[4] >> 28) & 0x0F
257	        # Word 5: {7'd0, self_test_busy, 8'd0, self_test_detail[7:0],
258	        #           3'd0, self_test_flags[4:0]}
259	        sr.self_test_flags = words[5] & 0x1F
260	        sr.self_test_detail = (words[5] >> 8) & 0xFF
261	        sr.self_test_busy = (words[5] >> 24) & 0x01
262	        return sr
263	
264	    @staticmethod
265	    def find_packet_boundaries(buf: bytes) -> list[tuple[int, int, str]]:
266	        """
267	        Scan buffer for packet start markers (0xAA data, 0xBB status).
268	        Returns list of (start_idx, expected_end_idx, packet_type).
269	        """
270	        packets = []
271	        i = 0
272	        while i < len(buf):
273	            if buf[i] == HEADER_BYTE:
274	                end = i + DATA_PACKET_SIZE
275	                if end <= len(buf) and buf[end - 1] == FOOTER_BYTE:
276	                    packets.append((i, end, "data"))
277	                    i = end
278	                else:
279	                    if end > len(buf):
280	                        break  # partial packet at end — leave for residual
281	                    i += 1  # footer mismatch — skip this false header
282	            elif buf[i] == STATUS_HEADER_BYTE:
283	                end = i + STATUS_PACKET_SIZE
284	                if end <= len(buf) and buf[end - 1] == FOOTER_BYTE:
285	                    packets.append((i, end, "status"))
286	                    i = end
287	                else:
288	                    if end > len(buf):
289	                        break  # partial status packet — leave for residual
290	                    i += 1  # footer mismatch — skip
291	            else:
292	                i += 1
293	        return packets
294	
295	
296	# ============================================================================
297	# FT2232H USB 2.0 Connection (pyftdi, 245 Synchronous FIFO)
298	# ============================================================================
299	
300	# Optional pyftdi import
301	try:
302	    from pyftdi.ftdi import Ftdi, FtdiError
303	    PyFtdi = Ftdi
304	    PYFTDI_AVAILABLE = True
305	except ImportError:
306	    class FtdiError(Exception):
307	        """Fallback FTDI error type when pyftdi is unavailable."""
308	
309	    PYFTDI_AVAILABLE = False
310	
311	
312	class FT2232HConnection:
313	    """
314	    FT2232H USB 2.0 Hi-Speed FIFO bridge communication.
315	    Uses pyftdi in 245 Synchronous FIFO mode (Channel A).
316	    VID:PID = 0x0403:0x6010 (FTDI default for FT2232H).
317	    """
318	
319	    VID = 0x0403
320	    PID = 0x6010
321	
322	    def __init__(self, mock: bool = True):
323	        self._mock = mock
324	        self._ftdi = None
325	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
326	        self.is_open = False
327	        # Mock state
328	        self._mock_frame_num = 0
329	        self._mock_rng = np.random.RandomState(42)
330	
331	    def open(self, device_index: int = 0) -> bool:
332	        if self._mock:
333	            self.is_open = True
334	            log.info("FT2232H mock device opened (no hardware)")
335	            return True
336	
337	        if not PYFTDI_AVAILABLE:
338	            log.error("pyftdi not installed — cannot open real FT2232H device")
339	            return False
340	
341	        try:
342	            self._ftdi = PyFtdi()
343	            url = f"ftdi://0x{self.VID:04x}:0x{self.PID:04x}/{device_index + 1}"
344	            self._ftdi.open_from_url(url)
345	            # Configure for 245 Synchronous FIFO mode
346	            self._ftdi.set_bitmode(0xFF, PyFtdi.BitMode.SYNCFF)
347	            # Set USB transfer size for throughput
348	            self._ftdi.read_data_set_chunksize(65536)
349	            self._ftdi.write_data_set_chunksize(65536)
350	            # Purge buffers
351	            self._ftdi.purge_buffers()
352	            self.is_open = True
353	            log.info(f"FT2232H device opened: {url}")
354	            return True
355	        except FtdiError as e:
356	            log.error(f"FT2232H open failed: {e}")
357	            return False
358	
359	    def close(self):
360	        if self._ftdi is not None:
361	            with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
362	                self._ftdi.close()
363	            self._ftdi = None
364	        self.is_open = False
365	
366	    def read(self, size: int = 4096) -> bytes | None:
367	        """Read raw bytes from FT2232H. Returns None on error/timeout."""
368	        if not self.is_open:
369	            return None
370	
371	        if self._mock:
372	            return self._mock_read(size)
373	
374	        with self._lock:
375	            try:
376	                data = self._ftdi.read_data(size)
377	                return bytes(data) if data else None
378	            except FtdiError as e:
379	                log.error(f"FT2232H read error: {e}")
380	                return None
381	
382	    def write(self, data: bytes) -> bool:
383	        """Write raw bytes to FT2232H (4-byte commands)."""
384	        if not self.is_open:
385	            return False
386	
387	        if self._mock:
388	            log.info(f"FT2232H mock write: {data.hex()}")
389	            return True
390	
391	        with self._lock:
392	            try:
393	                written = self._ftdi.write_data(data)
394	                return written == len(data)
395	            except FtdiError as e:
396	                log.error(f"FT2232H write error: {e}")
397	                return False
398	
399	    def _mock_read(self, size: int) -> bytes:
400	        """
401	        Generate synthetic 11-byte radar data packets for testing.
402	        Emits packets in sequential FPGA order (range_bin 0..63, doppler_bin
403	        0..31 within each range bin) so that RadarAcquisition._ingest_sample()
404	        places them correctly.  A target is injected near range bin 20,
405	        Doppler bin 8.
406	        """
407	        time.sleep(0.05)
408	        self._mock_frame_num += 1
409	
410	        buf = bytearray()
411	        num_packets = min(NUM_CELLS, size // DATA_PACKET_SIZE)
412	        start_idx = getattr(self, '_mock_seq_idx', 0)
413	
414	        for n in range(num_packets):
415	            idx = (start_idx + n) % NUM_CELLS
416	            rbin = idx // NUM_DOPPLER_BINS
417	            dbin = idx % NUM_DOPPLER_BINS
418	
419	            range_i = int(self._mock_rng.normal(0, 100))
420	            range_q = int(self._mock_rng.normal(0, 100))
421	            if abs(rbin - 20) < 3:
422	                range_i += 5000
423	                range_q += 3000
424	
425	            dop_i = int(self._mock_rng.normal(0, 50))
426	            dop_q = int(self._mock_rng.normal(0, 50))
427	            if abs(rbin - 20) < 3 and abs(dbin - 8) < 2:
428	                dop_i += 8000
429	                dop_q += 4000
430	
431	            detection = 1 if (abs(rbin - 20) < 2 and abs(dbin - 8) < 2) else 0
432	
433	            # Build compact 11-byte packet
434	            pkt = bytearray()
435	            pkt.append(HEADER_BYTE)
436	            pkt += struct.pack(">h", np.clip(range_q, -32768, 32767))
437	            pkt += struct.pack(">h", np.clip(range_i, -32768, 32767))
438	            pkt += struct.pack(">h", np.clip(dop_i, -32768, 32767))
439	            pkt += struct.pack(">h", np.clip(dop_q, -32768, 32767))
440	            # Bit 7 = frame_start (sample_counter == 0), bit 0 = detection
441	            det_byte = (detection & 0x01) | (0x80 if idx == 0 else 0x00)
442	            pkt.append(det_byte)
443	            pkt.append(FOOTER_BYTE)
444	
445	            buf += pkt
446	
447	        self._mock_seq_idx = (start_idx + num_packets) % NUM_CELLS
448	        return bytes(buf)
449	
450	
451	# ============================================================================
452	# FT601 USB 3.0 Connection (premium board only)
453	# ============================================================================
454	
455	# Optional ftd3xx import (FTDI's proprietary driver for FT60x USB 3.0 chips).
456	# pyftdi does NOT support FT601 — it only handles USB 2.0 chips (FT232H, etc.)
457	try:
458	    import ftd3xx  # type: ignore[import-untyped]
459	    FTD3XX_AVAILABLE = True
460	    _Ftd3xxError: type = ftd3xx.FTD3XXError  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
461	except ImportError:
462	    FTD3XX_AVAILABLE = False
463	    _Ftd3xxError = OSError  # fallback for type-checking; never raised
464	
465	
466	class FT601Connection:
467	    """
468	    FT601 USB 3.0 SuperSpeed FIFO bridge — premium board only.
469	
470	    The FT601 has a 32-bit data bus and runs at 100 MHz.
471	    VID:PID = 0x0403:0x6030 or 0x6031 (FTDI FT60x).
472	
473	    Requires the ``ftd3xx`` library (``pip install ftd3xx`` on Windows,
474	    or ``libft60x`` on Linux). This is FTDI's proprietary USB 3.0 driver;
475	    ``pyftdi`` only supports USB 2.0 and will NOT work with FT601.
476	
477	    Public contract matches FT2232HConnection so callers can swap freely.
478	    """
479	
480	    VID = 0x0403
481	    PID_LIST: ClassVar[list[int]] = [0x6030, 0x6031]
482	
483	    def __init__(self, mock: bool = True):
484	        self._mock = mock
485	        self._dev = None
486	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
487	        self.is_open = False
488	        # Mock state (reuses same synthetic data pattern)
489	        self._mock_frame_num = 0
490	        self._mock_rng = np.random.RandomState(42)
491	
492	    def open(self, device_index: int = 0) -> bool:
493	        if self._mock:
494	            self.is_open = True
495	            log.info("FT601 mock device opened (no hardware)")
496	            return True
497	
498	        if not FTD3XX_AVAILABLE:
499	            log.error(
500	                "ftd3xx library required for FT601 hardware — "
501	                "install with: pip install ftd3xx"
502	            )
503	            return False
504	
505	        try:
506	            self._dev = ftd3xx.create(device_index, ftd3xx.OPEN_BY_INDEX)
507	            if self._dev is None:
508	                log.error("No FT601 device found at index %d", device_index)
509	                return False
510	            # Verify chip configuration — only reconfigure if needed.
511	            # setChipConfiguration triggers USB re-enumeration, which
512	            # invalidates the device handle and requires a re-open cycle.
513	            cfg = self._dev.getChipConfiguration()
514	            needs_reconfig = (
515	                cfg.FIFOMode != 0            # 245 FIFO mode
516	                or cfg.ChannelConfig != 0    # 1 channel, 32-bit
517	                or cfg.OptionalFeatureSupport != 0
518	            )
519	            if needs_reconfig:
520	                cfg.FIFOMode = 0
521	                cfg.ChannelConfig = 0
522	                cfg.OptionalFeatureSupport = 0
523	                self._dev.setChipConfiguration(cfg)
524	                # Device re-enumerates — close stale handle, wait, re-open
525	                self._dev.close()
526	                self._dev = None
527	                import time
528	                time.sleep(2.0)  # wait for USB re-enumeration
529	                self._dev = ftd3xx.create(device_index, ftd3xx.OPEN_BY_INDEX)
530	                if self._dev is None:
531	                    log.error("FT601 not found after reconfiguration")
532	                    return False
533	                log.info("FT601 reconfigured and re-opened (index %d)", device_index)
534	            self.is_open = True
535	            log.info("FT601 device opened (index %d)", device_index)
536	            return True
537	        except (OSError, _Ftd3xxError) as e:
538	            log.error("FT601 open failed: %s", e)
539	            self._dev = None
540	            return False
541	
542	    def close(self):
543	        if self._dev is not None:
544	            with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
545	                self._dev.close()
546	            self._dev = None
547	        self.is_open = False
548	
549	    def read(self, size: int = 4096) -> bytes | None:
550	        """Read raw bytes from FT601. Returns None on error/timeout."""
551	        if not self.is_open:
552	            return None
553	
554	        if self._mock:
555	            return self._mock_read(size)
556	
557	        with self._lock:
558	            try:
559	                data = self._dev.readPipe(0x82, size, raw=True)
560	                return bytes(data) if data else None
561	            except (OSError, _Ftd3xxError) as e:
562	                log.error("FT601 read error: %s", e)
563	                return None
564	
565	    def write(self, data: bytes) -> bool:
566	        """Write raw bytes to FT601. Data must be 4-byte aligned for 32-bit bus."""
567	        if not self.is_open:
568	            return False
569	
570	        if self._mock:
571	            log.info(f"FT601 mock write: {data.hex()}")
572	            return True
573	
574	        # Pad to 4-byte alignment (FT601 32-bit bus requirement).
575	        # NOTE: Radar commands are already 4 bytes, so this should be a no-op.
576	        remainder = len(data) % 4
577	        if remainder:
578	            data = data + b"\x00" * (4 - remainder)
579	
580	        with self._lock:
581	            try:
582	                written = self._dev.writePipe(0x02, data, raw=True)
583	                return written == len(data)
584	            except (OSError, _Ftd3xxError) as e:
585	                log.error("FT601 write error: %s", e)
586	                return False
587	
588	    def _mock_read(self, size: int) -> bytes:
589	        """Generate synthetic radar packets (same pattern as FT2232H mock)."""
590	        time.sleep(0.05)
591	        self._mock_frame_num += 1
592	
593	        buf = bytearray()
594	        num_packets = min(NUM_CELLS, size // DATA_PACKET_SIZE)
595	        start_idx = getattr(self, "_mock_seq_idx", 0)
596	
597	        for n in range(num_packets):
598	            idx = (start_idx + n) % NUM_CELLS
599	            rbin = idx // NUM_DOPPLER_BINS
600	            dbin = idx % NUM_DOPPLER_BINS
601	
602	            range_i = int(self._mock_rng.normal(0, 100))
603	            range_q = int(self._mock_rng.normal(0, 100))
604	            if abs(rbin - 20) < 3:
605	                range_i += 5000
606	                range_q += 3000
607	
608	            dop_i = int(self._mock_rng.normal(0, 50))
609	            dop_q = int(self._mock_rng.normal(0, 50))
610	            if abs(rbin - 20) < 3 and abs(dbin - 8) < 2:
611	                dop_i += 8000
612	                dop_q += 4000
613	
614	            detection = 1 if (abs(rbin - 20) < 2 and abs(dbin - 8) < 2) else 0
615	
616	            pkt = bytearray()
617	            pkt.append(HEADER_BYTE)
618	            pkt += struct.pack(">h", np.clip(range_q, -32768, 32767))
619	            pkt += struct.pack(">h", np.clip(range_i, -32768, 32767))
620	            pkt += struct.pack(">h", np.clip(dop_i, -32768, 32767))
621	            pkt += struct.pack(">h", np.clip(dop_q, -32768, 32767))
622	            # Bit 7 = frame_start (sample_counter == 0), bit 0 = detection
623	            det_byte = (detection & 0x01) | (0x80 if idx == 0 else 0x00)
624	            pkt.append(det_byte)
625	            pkt.append(FOOTER_BYTE)
626	
627	            buf += pkt
628	
629	        self._mock_seq_idx = (start_idx + num_packets) % NUM_CELLS
630	        return bytes(buf)
631	
632	
633	
634	
635	
636	# ============================================================================
637	# Data Recorder (HDF5)
638	# ============================================================================
639	
640	try:
641	    import h5py
642	    HDF5_AVAILABLE = True
643	except ImportError:
644	    HDF5_AVAILABLE = False
645	
646	
647	class DataRecorder:
648	    """Record radar frames to HDF5 files for offline analysis."""
649	
650	    def __init__(self):
651	        self._file = None
652	        self._grp = None
653	        self._frame_count = 0
654	        self._recording = False
655	
656	    @property
657	    def recording(self) -> bool:
658	        return self._recording
659	
660	    def start(self, filepath: str):
661	        if not HDF5_AVAILABLE:
662	            log.error("h5py not installed — HDF5 recording unavailable")
663	            return
664	        try:
665	            self._file = h5py.File(filepath, "w")
666	            self._file.attrs["creator"] = "AERIS-10 Radar Dashboard"
667	            self._file.attrs["start_time"] = time.time()
668	            self._file.attrs["range_bins"] = NUM_RANGE_BINS
669	            self._file.attrs["doppler_bins"] = NUM_DOPPLER_BINS
670	
671	            self._grp = self._file.create_group("frames")
672	            self._frame_count = 0
673	            self._recording = True
674	            log.info(f"Recording started: {filepath}")
675	        except (OSError, ValueError) as e:
676	            log.error(f"Failed to start recording: {e}")
677	
678	    def record_frame(self, frame: RadarFrame):
679	        if not self._recording or self._file is None:
680	            return
681	        try:
682	            fg = self._grp.create_group(f"frame_{self._frame_count:06d}")
683	            fg.attrs["timestamp"] = frame.timestamp
684	            fg.attrs["frame_number"] = frame.frame_number
685	            fg.attrs["detection_count"] = frame.detection_count
686	            fg.create_dataset("magnitude", data=frame.magnitude, compression="gzip")
687	            fg.create_dataset("range_doppler_i", data=frame.range_doppler_i, compression="gzip")
688	            fg.create_dataset("range_doppler_q", data=frame.range_doppler_q, compression="gzip")
689	            fg.create_dataset("detections", data=frame.detections, compression="gzip")
690	            fg.create_dataset("range_profile", data=frame.range_profile, compression="gzip")
691	            self._frame_count += 1
692	        except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError) as e:
693	            log.error(f"Recording error: {e}")
694	
695	    def stop(self):
696	        if self._file is not None:
697	            try:
698	                self._file.attrs["end_time"] = time.time()
699	                self._file.attrs["total_frames"] = self._frame_count
700	                self._file.close()
701	            except (OSError, ValueError, RuntimeError):
702	                pass
703	            self._file = None
704	        self._recording = False
705	        log.info(f"Recording stopped ({self._frame_count} frames)")
706	
707	
708	# ============================================================================
709	# Radar Data Acquisition Thread
710	# ============================================================================
711	
712	class RadarAcquisition(threading.Thread):
713	    """
714	    Background thread: reads from USB (FT2232H), parses 11-byte packets,
715	    assembles frames, and pushes complete frames to the display queue.
716	    """
717	
718	    def __init__(self, connection, frame_queue: queue.Queue,
719	                 recorder: DataRecorder | None = None,
720	                 status_callback=None):
721	        super().__init__(daemon=True)
722	        self.conn = connection
723	        self.frame_queue = frame_queue
724	        self.recorder = recorder
725	        self._status_callback = status_callback
726	        self._stop_event = threading.Event()
727	        self._frame = RadarFrame()
728	        self._sample_idx = 0
729	        self._frame_num = 0
730	
731	    def stop(self):
732	        self._stop_event.set()
733	
734	    def run(self):
735	        log.info("Acquisition thread started")
736	        residual = b""
737	        while not self._stop_event.is_set():
738	            chunk = self.conn.read(4096)
739	            if chunk is None or len(chunk) == 0:
740	                time.sleep(0.01)
741	                continue
742	
743	            raw = residual + chunk
744	            packets = RadarProtocol.find_packet_boundaries(raw)
745	
746	            # Keep unparsed tail bytes for next iteration
747	            if packets:
748	                last_end = packets[-1][1]
749	                residual = raw[last_end:]
750	            else:
751	                # No packets found — keep entire buffer as residual
752	                # but cap at 2x max packet size to avoid unbounded growth
753	                max_residual = 2 * max(DATA_PACKET_SIZE, STATUS_PACKET_SIZE)
754	                residual = raw[-max_residual:] if len(raw) > max_residual else raw
755	            for start, end, ptype in packets:
756	                if ptype == "data":
757	                    parsed = RadarProtocol.parse_data_packet(
758	                        raw[start:end])
759	                    if parsed is not None:
760	                        self._ingest_sample(parsed)
761	                elif ptype == "status":
762	                    status = RadarProtocol.parse_status_packet(raw[start:end])
763	                    if status is not None:
764	                        log.info(f"Status: mode={status.radar_mode} "
765	                                 f"stream={status.stream_ctrl}")
766	                        if status.self_test_busy or status.self_test_flags:
767	                            log.info(f"Self-test: busy={status.self_test_busy} "
768	                                     f"flags=0b{status.self_test_flags:05b} "
769	                                     f"detail=0x{status.self_test_detail:02X}")
770	                        if self._status_callback is not None:
771	                            try:
772	                                self._status_callback(status)
773	                            except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
774	                                log.error(f"Status callback error: {e}")
775	
776	        log.info("Acquisition thread stopped")
777	
778	    def _ingest_sample(self, sample: dict):
779	        """Place sample into current frame and emit when complete."""
780	        rbin = self._sample_idx // NUM_DOPPLER_BINS
781	        dbin = self._sample_idx % NUM_DOPPLER_BINS
782	
783	        if rbin < NUM_RANGE_BINS and dbin < NUM_DOPPLER_BINS:
784	            self._frame.range_doppler_i[rbin, dbin] = sample["doppler_i"]
785	            self._frame.range_doppler_q[rbin, dbin] = sample["doppler_q"]
786	            mag = abs(int(sample["doppler_i"])) + abs(int(sample["doppler_q"]))
787	            self._frame.magnitude[rbin, dbin] = mag
788	            if sample.get("detection", 0):
789	                self._frame.detections[rbin, dbin] = 1
790	                self._frame.detection_count += 1
791	            # Accumulate FPGA range profile data (matched-filter output)
792	            # Each sample carries the range_i/range_q for this range bin.
793	            # Accumulate magnitude across Doppler bins for the range profile.
794	            ri = int(sample.get("range_i", 0))
795	            rq = int(sample.get("range_q", 0))
796	            self._frame.range_profile[rbin] += abs(ri) + abs(rq)
797	
798	        self._sample_idx += 1
799	
800	        if self._sample_idx >= NUM_CELLS:
801	            self._finalize_frame()
802	
803	    def _finalize_frame(self):
804	        """Complete frame: push to queue, record."""
805	        self._frame.timestamp = time.time()
806	        self._frame.frame_number = self._frame_num
807	        # range_profile is already accumulated from FPGA range_i/range_q
808	        # data in _ingest_sample(). No need to synthesize from doppler magnitude.
809	
810	        # Push to display queue (drop old if backed up)
811	        try:
812	            self.frame_queue.put_nowait(self._frame)
813	        except queue.Full:
814	            with contextlib.suppress(queue.Empty):
815	                self.frame_queue.get_nowait()
816	            self.frame_queue.put_nowait(self._frame)
817	
818	        if self.recorder and self.recorder.recording:
819	            self.recorder.record_frame(self._frame)
820	
821	        self._frame_num += 1
822	        self._frame = RadarFrame()
823	        self._sample_idx = 0
824
